description: Pattern catalogue for the scan-git-add PreToolUse hook. Maps 1:1 to PLAN.md §3 threat-model addendum. The hook ALSO reuses every pattern from hooks/patterns/scan-edit-write.json — applied to the content of each path being staged. The patterns below are scan-git-add-specific: tuned for diff context (added lines, added paths) and for secret-detection heuristics that only make sense at the index boundary.
skips_note: Snapshot test files (*.snap, /__snapshots__/) and lock files routinely contain high-entropy strings that aren't secrets — entropy + base64-prefix heuristics false-positive on them constantly. file-level path checks (.env*, secrets/, credentials/) still apply to lockfiles; entropy and high-confidence regexes (AKIA, github_pat_) still apply too. Only the entropy/heuristic check is skipped for lockfiles.

patterns.name: env-file-added
patterns.description: Adding a .env* file under any path
patterns.rationale: Environment files contain secrets and must never enter version control. This duplicates block-env-commit (the hook that intercepts the Bash command directly) as defence-in-depth: even if the operator confirms past that hook somehow, this one re-checks at the index boundary.

patterns.name: secrets-directory-added
patterns.description: Adding a file under a `secrets/` or `credentials/` directory
patterns.rationale: Directories named secrets/ or credentials/ are reserved conventions for unversioned material. Stage to a different path if the file is genuinely safe.

patterns.name: aws-access-key-added-line
patterns.description: AWS access key identifier (AKIA / ASIA prefix) in an added line
patterns.rationale: AWS credential leak. Rotate the key immediately if this was a real one, then move credentials to a secret manager.

patterns.name: github-fine-grained-pat-added-line
patterns.description: GitHub fine-grained PAT in an added line
patterns.rationale: GitHub credential leak. Revoke the PAT in the GitHub UI immediately.

patterns.name: private-key-block-added-line
patterns.description: Private key header (RSA/OPENSSH/EC/DSA/PGP/ED25519) in an added line
patterns.rationale: Private key material must not be committed. Move it to a secret manager and load it at runtime.

patterns.name: high-entropy-run-added-line
patterns.description: Run of ≥ 32 base64-ish characters with Shannon entropy ≥ 4.5 on an added line that is not a comment
patterns.rationale: Looks like an opaque token or generated credential. If it's a legitimate hash/UUID/test fixture, confirm; otherwise refuse and use an env var.
